what_it_does: Conceptual design document that reframes the embedder surface as a playable, dual-interface instrument for humans and MCP agents, built around multi-layer, re-embeddable frame-based readings of items.
observations: Declares the surface is an instrument to be used/played, not merely a projection viewer.
Proposes a dual interface: every capability has a visual face for Tim and an MCP tool face for agents over one engine.
Introduces a multi-layer model where one item can accumulate many layers, each a reading under a chosen frame.
Defines a layer as (item) + (embedder · context-frame · resolution · params · provenance) producing vector(s).
Lists layer operations: read/re-read, override, store, stack/compare/overlay, promote.
Frames centre, poles, context-parent, resolution, embedder, and time as all instances of the operator choosing a frame.
Resolves an earlier tension by declaring the instrument authors data; safety is git-revert, not consent.
Treats loading and filtering data as first-class authoring operations.
Holds all affordances to a FORM bar: minimal text, paper-aesthetic, spatial, non-developer UI.
standouts: The instrument authors; the read-only/consent framing is explicitly dissolved.
Layers as persistent frame-choices / coordinate systems over the same item origin.
The dual-face rule: a capability is not done until it exists on both the visual and MCP faces.
conventions: Living doc format with dated think-aloud attribution and verbatim quotes.
Internal wiki-style links such as [[collective-cognition]] and [[project-address-accumulation]].
References sibling files in the embedder-pplx set (VISION-OVERVIEW.md, MODEL-CAPABILITIES-AS-AFFORDANCES.md, DATA-SUBSTRATE-POSTGRES-SUPABASE.md).
Section numbering and bold defined terms.
concerns: Open questions remain unresolved: layer identity/slots, default layer visibility, scope of store redesign.
The mix of settled decisions and open forks could be read as a final spec.
Dense conceptual language may be hard to implement without a concrete schema.
notes: This is the conceptual heart of the embedder-pplx set: it turns the projection surface into an authorable, multi-layer instrument shared by a human and agents.
questions: What is the exact schema/identity model for a layer (slot-based vs free stack with current pointer)?
How does the MCP tool registry map one-to-one to visual affordances in the UI?
Where is the precise boundary between filtering (read-only subset) and framing (authoring a new layer)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it states it is a living conceptual doc for what the instrument is becoming and does exactly that.
intention_signals: To establish a shared conceptual model where embedding is not a one-time vectorization but an ongoing, operator-driven accumulation of framed readings, accessible to both humans and agents through a single registry of verbs.
novelty: Treats embeddings as a stratigraphy of persistent, re-embeddable, frame-bound readings (layers) rather than a single fixed vector per item, and insists every capability has both a human-visual and an MCP-tool face.
contribution: A conceptual vocabulary (instrument, dual interface, layer, frame, override/store/promote) and a decision that the surface authors data, which a fused system would keep as the organizing model for embedding interactions.
summary_for_embedding: A conceptual design document defining the embedder surface as a dual-interface instrument for humans and MCP agents, centered on multi-layer, re-embeddable frame-based readings of items.
intention_for_embedding: To settle the conceptual model and authoring stance for the embedder-pplx instrument so that capabilities can be built as shared human/agent verbs over accumulated layers.
